Bulk RNA-seq deconvolution with a (matched or external) scRNAseq reference

Source: R/extensions.R
SpaCET.deconvolution.matched.scRNAseq.for.bulk.Rd

Deconvolve bulk RNA-seq samples against a single-cell reference using the matched-scRNAseq pipeline. Each bulk sample is treated as an independent mixture and solved jointly via hierarchical constrained NNLS against the reference. Algorithmic semantics are identical to SpaCET.deconvolution.matched.scRNAseq with sc_includeMalignant=TRUE: no malignant-first CNA cascade, no within-cohort purity rescaling, malignant cells deconvolved jointly with non-malignant types when present in the reference.

Usage

SpaCET.deconvolution.matched.scRNAseq.for.bulk(
  counts,
  sc_counts,
  sc_annotation,
  sc_lineageTree,
  sc_downsampling = TRUE,
  sc_nCellEachLineage = 100,
  coreNo = 6
)

Arguments

counts

Bulk count matrix with gene name (row) x sample ID (column). Raw counts are expected (the function applies its own normalization internally, the same as the spatial pipeline).

sc_counts

Single cell count matrix with gene name (row) x cell ID (column).

sc_annotation

Single cell annotation matrix. Must include two columns named cellID and cellType. Each row represents a single cell.

sc_lineageTree

Cell lineage tree. A named list whose names are major lineages and whose values are vectors of sub-lineages. If a major lineage has no sub-lineages, the value should be the major lineage name itself.

sc_downsampling

Whether to downsample the single-cell data per cell type before building the reference. Default: TRUE.

sc_nCellEachLineage

Cell count per lineage when downsampling. Default: 100. If a cell type has more than this many cells, that many are randomly selected (with seed 123) to build the reference.

coreNo

Core number for parallel reference construction.

Value

A SpaCET object whose @results$deconvolution$propMat slot holds the cell-type proportion matrix (cell types x samples). The object has placeholder spatial coordinates and a platform = "Bulk" flag, so visualization functions that depend on real coordinates should not be used; downstream summarization should access propMat directly.

Details

This function targets the unmatched-bulk use case (e.g. TCGA cohorts deconvolved against an external scRNAseq atlas). The reference does not need to come from the same patients as the bulk samples — what matters is that it covers all relevant cell types at sufficient resolution. When reference and bulk come from different cohorts, set sc_downsampling=TRUE (default) and consider seeding the reference with a sub-lineage-aware lineage tree so cross-cohort variability does not collapse onto a single major-type signature.

This function intentionally exposes only the matched-scRNAseq path (sc_includeMalignant=TRUE) and does not call the malignant-first CNA cascade used by SpaCET.deconvolution for spatial spots. The per-sample CNA cascade is designed for the spot-scale (1-100 cells per spot) and is not appropriate for bulk RNA-seq, where each sample aggregates over millions of cells.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Bulk count matrix: genes x samples (e.g. TCGA-BRCA)
bulk_counts <- ...

# scRNAseq reference (e.g. Wu et al. 2021 BRCA atlas)
sc_counts <- ...        # genes x cells
sc_annotation <- ...    # cellID, cellType
lineageTree <- list(
  "Malignant" = "Malignant",
  "T cell"    = c("T CD4", "T CD8"),
  "Myeloid"   = c("Macrophage", "DC", "Monocyte"),
  "B cell"    = "B cell",
  "CAF"       = "CAF",
  "Endothelial" = "Endothelial"
)

propMat <- SpaCET.deconvolution.matched.scRNAseq.for.bulk(
  counts        = bulk_counts,
  sc_counts     = sc_counts,
  sc_annotation = sc_annotation,
  sc_lineageTree = lineageTree
)

} # }
